# MINISTRY OF HEALTH

THE WEEKLY EPIDEMIOLOGICAL BULLETIN

### WEEK 46: 11th Nov –18th Nov 2019

Dear Reader, We are pleased to share with you our 46th weekly epidemiological bulletin. This weekly epidemiological bulletin serves to inform all stakeholders at district, national, and global levels on disease trends, public health surveillance and interventions undertaken in detecting, preventing and responding

to public health events in the country on a weekly basis.

##### In this issue, we bring you the following Highlights

 Average national weekly surveillance reporting rate was at 79%

 Poor reporting have been observed in 10 districts of Mityana, Kiboga, Kiryandongo, Wakiso, Pader, Mukono, Iganga, Kitgum,Bugiri and Kotido.

 Malaria is the leading public health event (Page 1)

 9 maternal deaths were registered.

 157 perinatal deaths were registered.

For comments please contact: Dr. Allan Muruta, Commissioner, Department of Integrated Epidemiology, Surveillance and Public Health Emergencies - MoH; P.O BOX 7272 Kampala, Tel: 080010066 (toll free); Email: esd@health.go.ug or esduganda22@gmail.com

Week 46, the reporting rates were as follows;

 National rate was 78.9% more than 78.0% in week 45

 Central regional had 68.7% more than 68.2% in week 45

 Eastern region had 75.8% less than 76.3% in week 45

 Western was 85.7%) more than 85.5 in week 45

 Northern was (85.5%) less than 82.1% in Week 45, (Refer to Annex 1.).

 23% (31/135) of the districts achieved 100%

 54.8% (74/135) of the districts achieved the target of 80%

 7.4% (10/135) had reporting rates less than 60%.

|Current public health threats|
|---|


##### EVD

-  High risk districts continue EVD preparedness & prevention activities
-  Community based disease surveillance is on going


Malaria outbreak Currently 53 districts in 9 regions are responding to Malaria outbreaks

<!-- Page 2 -->

|Priority diseases|
|---|


The data shows high number of Typhoid, Malaria, Dysentery,

SARI and Animal bites cases in different parts of the country.

Table 1: Case Fatality Rate of Priority Diseases, week 46

|Conditions Cases Deaths<br><br>CFR (%)| |
|---|---|
|AFP 0 0 0.0 AEFIs 5 0 0.0 Animal bites 277 0 0.0 Bacterial Meningitis 3 1 33.3 Cholera 0 0 0.0 Dysentery 466 0 0.0 Guinea Worm 1 0 0.0 Malaria 33550 39 0.12 Presumptive MDR-TB 2 0 0.0 Measles 127 1 0.8 NNT 0 0 0.0 Plague 6 0 0.0 Other VHFs 0 0 0.0 SARI 205 1 0.5 Typhoid fever 1410 0 0.0 Yellow Fever 0 0 0.0<br><br>| |
| |Datasource:DHIS|


Datasource:DHIS

Measles: 113/128 district have investigated at least one sus-

pected measles case making it 88% while 15 out of 128 (12%)

district are still silent District. 39% 0f the measles suspects turned positive for measles IgM.

#### Maternal and Perinatal deaths:

There were 9 maternal deaths and 157 perinatal deaths in week 46 as summarised in table 3.

Table 3: Distribution of maternal deaths (n=9) and perinatal deaths (n=157), week 46

|District|No. of maternal deaths|Perinatal deaths district top 10|
|---|---|---|
|Kampala|1|53|
|Mbale|0|4|
|Kyotera|1|4|
|Nebbi|1|0|
|Moroto|1|1|
|Kasese|1|3|
|Masindi|1|0|
|Zombo|1|0|
|Mayuge|1|0|
|Hoima|0|15|
|Lira|0|12|
|Bwikwe|0|6|
|Mubende|0|6|
|Gulu|0|5|
|Kitgum|0|5|
|Kagadi|0|5|
|Other Districts|0|38|


Recommendations  Poor reporting districts should be helped to improve reporting rates.  All districts should strengthen surveillance activities in AFP, Measles and VHFs.  National Malaria Control Program needs support in strengthening malaria control and prevention

efforts.  The increasing number of suspected cases of Typhoid fever requires verification and investigation.  Districts and health facilities should be supported to diagnose Typhoid fever according to the Integrat-

ed Disease Surveillance and Response guidelines.  There is need to profile all animal bites in the country.  Maternal and Child health department should investigate the high numbers of maternal and perinatal

deaths.
